titleStmt.title: SII 9.64: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 64. (A.R. No. 75 of 1904.) ON THE THIRD SLAB SET UP ON THE WEST SIDE OF A RUINED TEMPLE CLOSE TO THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE AT BAGALI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.

summary.p: This is dated Śaka 868, Krōdhi; Krōdhin was Śaka 867 current = A.D. 94445. It refers itself to the reign of the Rāshṭrakūṭa king Kannaradēva (Kṛishṇa III) whose Mahāsāmanta Katyera of the Chāḷukya family was ruling over Kōgaḷi five-hundred and Māsiyavāḍi hundred-and-forty and Dōvayya was the Pergaḍe. A certain Ajavarma is said to have received a gift for the village Oḍḍavaḍaṅgila.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . sakanṛpakālādbhudayasaṁvatsarasataṅga Eṇṭunūra Aṟuvattu
Eṇṭaneya krōdhiyaṁba vatsaraṁ pravartise kannaradēva rājyaṁgeye
svatti samadhigatapaṁcamahāśabda mahāsamanta turagaveḍe
ṅga biḷiyaṁbināta nāhavasūdrakaṁ vidviṣṭamārttaṁḍa
priyagāḷḷa cāḷukyavaṁśōdbhavaṁ katyera kōga
ṟi Aynūru māsiyavadi nūṟanālvattu
manāḷuttamire samasthaguṇanidhi gōtra(nā)
nārāyaṇaṁ svajanavatsaḷaṁ svāminībhṛtyaṁ
śrīdōvayyaṁ perggaḍetanaṁgeE Oḍḍavaḍa
ṅgela Amṛta Ajavarmma ṟoṭṭabhuvaṇna Ajavarmma Ūrgge
paḍedoridanaḻidātaṁ kavileyuṁ vāraṇā
siyumanaṟido . svadattāṁ paradattānvā yō
harēta vasundhāri . ṣaṣṭivariṣasahasrā
ṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimi . sāmānyōyaṁ
dharmmasēturnṛpāṇāṁ kālē kālē pālanīyō
bhavadbhiḥ sarvānētānbhāginaḥ pārttivēndrānbhūyō
bhūyō yācatē rāmabhadra[ḥ .*]
Digital edition of SII 9.64 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
